D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

Benham's Spinning Disk

spin a black-and-white disk and watch faint pastel colours rise out of pure ink

The disk carries no pigment but black and white. Spinning flickers each radius on and off at its own rhythm, and the L, M, and S cones — recovering at different rates — disagree, painting illusory colour.

Slide the spin speed and fix your gaze on the centre. The arcs at different radii flush into warm reds and cool blues — colours that exist only in your retina, never in the ink.
